content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule. Looking at atom N:7 (nitrogen with index 7), I can see it is connected to:

1. C:1 (carbon atom with index 1)
2. C:6 (carbon atom with index 6)

Let me examine the connections more carefully. From the image, atom N:7 appears to be bonded to two carbon atoms (C:1 and C:6) and possibly one hydrogen atom. Let me check if there's a hydrogen atom attached to N:7.

Looking at the structure, I can see that N:7 has three bonds total:
- One bond to C:1
- One bond to C:6  
- One bond to H:18 (hydrogen atom with index 18)

So the neighbor atoms of N:7 are:
- C:1 (carbon)
- C:6 (carbon)
- H:18 (hydrogen)

The geomconf_index for this molecule appears to be 9158, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_9158.xyz".

Let me confirm this by checking if there are any other connections I might have missed.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 7, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
